choices.C: DaemonSets deploying one pod per node
choices.D: StatefulSets with a single replica and persistent volume claims

question: A company's Azure SQL Database is in a premium Business Critical tier. A read-heavy reporting workload is causing performance issues on the primary replica. What built-in Business Critical feature can offload the reporting workload without additional cost?

choices.A: Configure active geo-replication to a read replica in the same region
choices.B: Use the built-in read-only secondary replica (Read Scale-Out) via ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly in the connection string
choices.C: Deploy a separate Azure SQL Database for reporting with manual data sync
choices.D: Enable elastic pool with reporting-specific eDTU allocation

question: Your organization's DR strategy requires that all critical Azure workloads can be recovered within 2 hours in a separate Azure region. You use Azure Backup and Azure Site Recovery. What is the most important practice to validate readiness?

choices.A: Review Azure Backup completion status in Azure portal weekly
choices.B: Conduct regular DR drills: perform Test Failovers with ASR, validate restored backups, and measure actual RTO/RPO against targets
choices.C: Enable Cross-Region Restore on Recovery Services vaults
choices.D: Monitor ASR replication health metrics daily

question: A critical on-premises application is being migrated to Azure VMs. The application has a 4-hour RTO and 30-minute RPO. You select Azure Site Recovery (ASR). What replication frequency does ASR use for Azure VMs?

choices.A: Every 30 minutes — configurable snapshot frequency
choices.B: Continuous replication using change block tracking — RPO typically under 5 minutes for Azure-to-Azure
choices.C: Daily replication at midnight
choices.D: Every 15 minutes — minimum replication interval for ASR

question: Your company wants to ensure that even if the Azure subscription admin account is compromised and all backup data in a Recovery Services vault is deleted, the backups cannot be permanently lost for at least 7 days. Which vault feature provides this protection?

choices.A: Vault soft delete (14 days by default)
choices.B: Immutable vault with Azure Backup — prevents deletion operations and requires Multi-User Authorization (MUA) with a separate Resource Guard resource
choices.C: Geo-redundant vault storage
choices.D: Cross-region restore capability

question: Your Azure SQL Database General Purpose tier has 7-day automated backup retention. A critical table was corrupted 10 days ago due to a slow-running bad update. The corruption was discovered today. Can you recover the data?

choices.A: Yes — Azure SQL Database keeps all backups indefinitely
choices.B: No — the 7-day retention window does not cover 10 days ago; you need Long-Term Retention (LTR) or a longer retention period to recover from this scenario
choices.C: Yes — Azure SQL Database can recover from any point using transaction logs
choices.D: Yes — active geo-replication keeps a historical copy in the secondary

question: You are implementing business continuity for an Azure Cosmos DB workload. The compliance requirement states that data loss must not exceed 15 minutes under any failure scenario. Which Cosmos DB configuration meets this requirement?

choices.A: Single-region account with periodic backup mode (backup every 4 hours)
choices.B: Multi-region account with automatic failover and at least one additional read region — replication lag typically seconds; worst-case bounded staleness covers up to the configured K/T parameters
choices.C: Single-region account with continuous backup mode (30-day PITR)
choices.D: Serverless account with geo-redundant backup

question: Your organization deploys Azure App Service web apps in two regions (East US primary, West Europe secondary) using Azure Traffic Manager with Priority routing. The East US App Service goes down. DNS TTL is set to 60 seconds. What is the approximate time before users are routed to West Europe?

choices.A: Instant — Traffic Manager uses anycast for immediate routing
choices.B: Approximately 60-120 seconds — Traffic Manager detects the failure via health probes, waits for DNS TTL to expire, then clients get the West Europe IP
choices.C: 30 minutes — Traffic Manager's default failover grace period
choices.D: Immediate — Traffic Manager updates DNS A records in real time

question: Your organization runs a critical workload on Azure VMs in East US. A full regional failure occurs and ASR fails over to West Europe. The workload is now running in West Europe. Once East US recovers, you want to return to East US as primary. What ASR operation enables this?

choices.A: Initiate a second unplanned failover back to East US
choices.B: Re-protect the VMs (reverses replication direction to East US), then perform a planned failover back to East US
choices.C: Delete the VMs in West Europe and redeploy in East US
choices.D: Disable ASR and set up new replication from West Europe to East US

question: A company stores sensitive backup data in a Recovery Services vault. Compliance requires that no single administrator can disable backup protection or delete backup data without approval from a second administrator. Which Azure feature enforces this dual-approval requirement?

choices.A: Azure RBAC with two separate Owner accounts
choices.B: Multi-User Authorization (MUA) with an Azure Backup Resource Guard in a separate subscription
choices.C: PIM with approval workflow for the Backup Operator role
choices.D: Azure Policy Deny effect on vault deletion operations

question: You need to design a BCDR solution for an Azure SQL Database where both RTO and RPO must be under 1 minute for a same-region hardware failure. The application connection string must not change. Which configuration meets this requirement?

choices.A: Azure SQL Database General Purpose with auto-failover groups
choices.B: Azure SQL Database Business Critical tier — built-in Always On AG provides automatic failover within seconds for local hardware failures with near-zero data loss
choices.C: Azure SQL Database active geo-replication with manual failover
choices.D: Azure SQL Managed Instance with auto-failover group

question: Your disaster recovery runbook requires that Azure VMs in the DR region are started in a specific order: database VMs first, then application VMs, then web VMs. Which ASR feature automates this sequencing during failover?

choices.A: ASR replication frequency settings
choices.B: ASR Recovery Plans with groups — database VMs in Group 1, app VMs in Group 2, web VMs in Group 3, with scripts/manual steps between groups
choices.C: Azure Automation runbook triggered after each VM starts
choices.D: VMSS with ordered instance start configurations

question: A financial services company needs to meet an RPO of near zero (seconds) and RTO of under 2 minutes for their Azure SQL Database workload, including automatic failover with no manual intervention. Which combination provides the highest level of protection?

choices.A: SQL Database General Purpose + Azure Backup with 15-minute PITR
choices.B: SQL Database Business Critical tier + Auto-failover group — Business Critical provides near-zero RPO via synchronous replicas and auto-failover group provides automatic regional failover with stable DNS endpoint
choices.C: SQL Database Hyperscale + active geo-replication
choices.D: SQL Managed Instance + geo-redundant backups
